summary: Added one more deterministic UI fixture for Today Continue: a debug-only seed writes one active Home task through Home persistence, and the maintained XCUITest smoke now proves Today Continue can render source-derived Home work through stable accessibility identifiers.

contract_status_changes.before: Owlory UI smoke had deterministic seeds for a fresh Today dashboard and one current Focus-backed Continue item.
contract_status_changes.after: Owlory UI smoke also has --owlory-ui-seed-home-task-continue-item, which resets app-local data and writes one active Home task through the existing Home task repository.

contract_status_changes.contract: Today Continue source-derived UI smoke coverage
contract_status_changes.before: The maintained XCUITest smoke proved Today launch and one current Focus-backed Continue row only.
contract_status_changes.after: The maintained XCUITest smoke also proves that an active Home task source renders in Today Continue through the existing source-derived Continue projection.

contract_status_changes.before: The docs described deterministic UI smoke coverage for the Today launch surface and one seeded Focus-backed Continue item.
contract_status_changes.after: The docs now describe the added active Home-task-backed Continue fixture and keep the proof claim limited to focused XCUITest smoke, not broad regression or screenshots.

residual_risks: This remains focused XCUITest smoke coverage, not a broad UI regression suite.
The seeded Home task proves only one active Home task source in Today Continue; it does not prove every Continue source, multi-screen routing, screenshots, device behavior, or TestFlight behavior.
No repo-managed screenshots were preserved, so screenshot-verified proof is not claimed.
Device and TestFlight behavior remain unverified.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop is valid. If more UI harness depth is needed later, queue one focused fixture and one proof at a time, such as a seeded Write note in the Write tab or an active Home protocol run in Today Continue.
